function.name: _app_get_define_list
function.description:
Get object structure metadata (Define Objects) in the current application definition.

【Call Prerequisites】
- Must first call _app_get_define_type to get a valid type
- Must not construct type values out of thin air

【Usage Scenarios】
- AI analysis of application structure
- Building dependency graphs
- Reading structure before automatically generating modification plans
- Structure comparison before publishing
- Draft and published version difference analysis

【Important Notes】
- Defaults to reading draft version
- Does not modify any data
- Return results are only used for structural analysis and decision-making

【Hierarchy Rules (Must be followed)】

1. App-level objects (scope fixed as `App`)
- ApiDefine
- ApplicationDefine
- ApplicationOptionDefine
- ModuleDefine

2. Module-level objects (scope must be the id of the corresponding ModuleDefine)
- BpmnProcessDefine
- DashboardCardDefine
- DashboardFilterDefine
- DataSourceDefine
- FormDesignerDefine
- PageDesignerDefine
- SurveyItemDefine
- TableFieldDefine
- TableFieldGroupDefine
- TableIndexDefine
- TextindexTableDefine
- WebsiteResourceDefine

【Mandatory Rules】
- If type belongs to App-level objects and scope ≠ `App`, execution is not allowed
- If type belongs to Module-level objects and no valid ModuleDefine id is provided, an error must be reported
- AI must not assume Module id on its own

type.description: Object type. Must come from the id field of the _app_get_define_type return value

scope.description:
Parent Scope Identifier.

- App-level objects: Must fill in "App"
- Module-level objects: Must fill in the id of the corresponding ModuleDefine
